skill.name: senior-devops
skill.description: DevOps guidance skill with reference material and simple Python CLI scripts for CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and deployment planning. It helps users structure reviews and produce local report output, but it does not provision resources or run deployments.
skill.summary: Plan CI/CD, infrastructure, and deployment work with DevOps references and local report scripts.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns. Review found the weak cryptography, reconnaissance, environment access, and Ruby backtick findings are documentation false positives. The real risk is medium because three Python CLI scripts overwrite a user-selected output file.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output File Overwrite
medium_findings.description: The three Python scripts write JSON results to the output option path. This can overwrite a local file if users provide a sensitive path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is directly present and uses the CLI-supplied output path. It is not malicious by itself, but the overwrite behavior is a real local filesystem risk.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Static Hits Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick locations are fenced markdown examples or placeholder TypeScript snippets. No Ruby backticks, subprocess calls, or shell execution were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain markdown code fences and sample commands for users to read. They are not executable code paths inside the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Hits Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts point to generic prose or headings. No cryptographic algorithm use, host enumeration, or data collection logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation text without cryptography or reconnaissance behavior. The scanner appears to have matched substrings instead of behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment File Access Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The environment file alert is a setup command that copies an example file. No code reads, parses, transmits, or exposes environment secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are a setup example, not automated secret access. No network code or environment variable reads were found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python File Write From CLI Argument
dangerous_patterns.description: Each script writes JSON output to the caller-provided output path. Users should choose a safe destination and avoid privileged files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write behavior is explicit and repeated across all three scripts. The risk is local file overwrite, not exfiltration or command execution.

content.user_title: Guide DevOps Pipeline and IaC Planning
content.value_statement: DevOps delivery often needs structured pipeline, infrastructure, and deployment guidance. This skill supplies reference guidance and simple Python report scripts for planning and review.
actual_capabilities: Explains CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and deployment planning topics from included reference files.
Provides three Python CLI entry points for pipeline, Terraform, and deployment report workflows.
Validates that a user-specified target path exists before producing script results.
Prints simple status reports and can output JSON results to a selected local file.
Lists common DevOps commands and workflow steps for planning and review.
limitations: The scripts are placeholders and do not generate real pipeline or Terraform files.
The skill does not deploy applications or provision cloud resources.
Generated reports contain basic status data, not deep infrastructure analysis.
Users must review any suggested commands before running them in live environments.

use_cases.title: Plan CI/CD workflow reviews
use_cases.description: Use the references to structure pipeline stages, quality checks, and release gates before implementation.

use_cases.title: Organize IaC planning sessions
use_cases.description: Map Terraform and cloud infrastructure discussion points before writing production modules.

use_cases.title: Standardize deployment discussions
use_cases.description: Create a shared checklist for deployment strategy, monitoring needs, and operational ownership.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Pipeline Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a CI/CD planning checklist for a small web service. Include build, test, security, release, and rollback topics.

prompt_templates.title: Deployment Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this deployment process and identify missing checks, risky manual steps, and monitoring gaps. Keep the recommendations practical.

prompt_templates.title: Terraform Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an infrastructure as code planning outline for this cloud application. Include networking, state, access control, and environment separation.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Platform Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this multi-service delivery workflow across CI/CD, Terraform, Kubernetes, observability, and rollback strategy. Prioritize operational risks.

output_examples.input: Create a CI/CD planning checklist for a Node.js API.
output: Pipeline stages grouped by build, test, security review, packaging, and release approval.
Questions for secrets handling, artifact storage, and environment promotion.
Manual review points for production deploys and rollback ownership.

output_examples.input: Review my Terraform rollout plan for a new service.
output: Infrastructure planning topics for state storage, module boundaries, networking, and permissions.
Risk notes about environment separation, drift detection, and change review.
A short list of decisions to confirm before implementation.

output_examples.input: Summarize deployment strategy options for a service team.
output: Comparison of rolling, blue-green, and canary release approaches.
Operational requirements for health checks, monitoring, and rollback triggers.
Recommendation prompts based on traffic pattern, risk tolerance, and team maturity.

best_practices: Provide the application stack, deployment target, and operational constraints before asking for guidance.
Treat generated checklists and reports as planning aids, not production-ready infrastructure changes.
Run included scripts only against intended paths and write output to a safe local file.
anti_patterns: Expecting the placeholder scripts to generate complete production Terraform or pipeline files.
Running documented deployment commands against a live cluster without a review step.
Writing script output to sensitive files or privileged system paths.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy infrastructure?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and simple local reporting scripts. It does not run Terraform, Kubernetes, or cloud provider actions.

faq.question: Does it execute shell commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed scripts do not call subprocesses or shell execution APIs.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The scripts can overwrite a user-selected output file. This is useful CLI behavior, but it needs user care.

faq.question: Can it access secrets from environment files?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The environment file reference is only a setup example in documentation.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with Python scripts and markdown references.

faq.question: What should users review before using it?
faq.answer: Review any command examples, target paths, and output file paths before using them in a real project.
